weekly.angle: A dialectical epistemological debate framed around a clinical mystery. Host A (pro-measurement/positivism) critiques Freud's bias and lack of 'hard data' in the Dora case, while Host B (pro-hermeneutics) defends 'psychic reality' and narrative coherence as rigorous empirical data using Ricoeur's validation criteria.
must_cover: The epistemological pivot from positivist 3rd-person trait models to hermeneutic interpretation of unconscious meaning.
Establishing 'Psykisk realitet' (psychic reality) as a scientifically legitimate empirical object distinct from material truth.
The practical methodological missteps, overdetermination of symptoms, and power dynamics (transference) in Freud's Dora case.
avoid: Getting bogged down in Ricoeur's abstract philosophical vocabulary without tethering it to Gammelgaard's synthesis.
Presenting psychoanalysis merely as an outdated historical artifact rather than an active, viable epistemological challenge to mainstream positivist psychology.
grounding: Constantly ground the discussion of hermeneutic criteria in the clinical reality and symptom puzzle of the Dora case.
Use Gammelgaard's 4 criteria (speech, intentionality, psychic reality, narrative) to structure Ricoeur's complex claims.

per_reading.angle: The philosophical defense of psychoanalysis. Focus on how facts are not observable behaviors but narrative 'reports' verified by a synergy of criteria.
must_cover: The necessity of combining the hermeneutic model of meaning with the economic model of force.
Verification relying on a cumulative synergy of partial criteria rather than standard empirical proof.
avoid: Over-explaining the dense philosophical critique of Habermas; keep focus on what makes a psychoanalytic fact valid.

per_reading.angle: The messy clinical application. Frame this as the primary empirical text demonstrating how psychoanalytic data is gathered and interpreted.
must_cover: The concepts of somatic compliance and overdetermination of hysterical symptoms.
The discovery and mishandling of transference.
The distinction between primary and secondary gain from illness.
avoid: Presenting Dora's case as a completed success; highlight that it was a fragment broken off due to Freud's failure to master the transference.

per_reading.angle: The pedagogical bridge. Use this to cleanly define psychoanalytic empiricism for a student audience.
must_cover: Psychoanalytic empiricism is defined by four criteria: the spoken word, intentionality, psychic reality, and narrative history.
Validation relies on narrative coherence and relational meaning rather than objective correspondence.
avoid: Treating Gammelgaard as entirely separate from Ricoeur; explicitly frame her text as the accessible synthesis of Ricoeur's framework.

per_slide.angle: The classroom synthesis on defense mechanisms and structural pathology.
must_cover: Psychoanalysis as simultaneously a treatment form and a scientific investigation.
How defense mechanisms start as adaptive and how their maturity defines personality structure (neurotic vs. borderline/psychotic).
Freud's clinical practice in the Dora case often contradicting his theoretical ideals due to patriarchal norms.
avoid: Getting lost in the myriad of defense mechanisms; focus on how they operate as 'resistance' in the collection of empirical data.

per_slide.angle: The practical method of psychoanalytic listening.
must_cover: The distinction between being 'anti-scientistic' (rejecting reductive positivism) and 'anti-scientific'.
Psychoanalytic empirical data isn't the literal words, but the meaning and intention behind them (form vs. content).
The verification problem of reasoning backwards from interpretation to cause.
avoid: Detailing the specific dictaphone/urine clinical example too heavily; stick to the methodological takeaway of 'how to listen'.

short.angle: A quick, punchy explainer on why 'Psychic Reality' matters just as much as 'Material Truth' in psychology.
must_cover: The difference between material reality (what objectively happened) and psychic reality (how the subject internalizes and structuralizes it as a fantasy).
Why psychoanalysis requires a different kind of empirical evidence (narrative coherence) than trait psychology.
avoid: Detailed historical overviews of Freud's life; keep it strictly conceptual and epistemological.

selected_concepts.role: The core epistemological object of psychoanalysis; validates subjective, unconscious inner worlds as legitimate empirical data.
Explains how the therapeutic relationship itself becomes empirical data by restaging the patient's history, as seen in the Dora case.
Crucial for understanding therapeutic resistance and the motives anchoring neuroses, contrasting internal psychological defense with external practical advantages.
Justifies the hermeneutic method of multi-layered interpretation; a single symptom can represent several unconscious processes.

selected_tensions.stakes: Defends psychoanalysis against critiques of being unscientific by arguing that demanding directly observable data would destroy the phenomenon of the unconscious.
Shifts the standard of 'facts' away from empirical observation toward the patient's subjective construction of meaning, framing the course's interpretive turn.
Highlights psychoanalysis's dual nature: treating symptoms as texts to decipher (hermeneutics) while handling resistance as a system of energies (economics).

grounding_notes: Ensure definitions of primary/secondary gain explicitly reflect Freud's 1923 internal/external distinction footnote.
All clinical claims regarding Dora must explicitly anchor back to Freud's text, maintaining the tension between his brilliance and his patriarchal blind spots.
Ricoeur's criteria for facts must be presented through Gammelgaard's four-part pedagogical breakdown to ensure student comprehension.

source_selection.why: Provides the foundational philosophical and epistemological defense of psychoanalysis as a unique science, defining the core debate of the lecture.
The primary clinical text (Dora case) demonstrating how theory is applied, how empirical data is gathered, and where methodological failures occur.
A crucial pedagogical synthesis that translates Ricoeur's dense epistemology into clear, testable criteria for students.
Connects the epistemological texts to broader personality theory concepts like defense mechanisms and structural pathology.
Clarifies the methodological distinction between anti-scientistic and anti-scientific, providing practical listening strategies.
